title: pub msg_type field on WebSocket types allows callers to set wrong @type discriminator
description: Scenario: caller constructs ChatStreamEnable::new(...) then sets .msg_type = 'ChatStreamDisable'. Server receives a message with wrong @type. The constructor sets it correctly but the field is pub so anyone can mutate it. Maintainer consequence: any future contributor adding a new WebSocket type will copy the pub field pattern and create the same footgun. Fix: make msg_type private with no accessor, or make it a typed const so the field cannot be set incorrectly. The constructor pattern is already correct — hiding the field removes the footgun.
close_reason: Made msg_type pub(crate) on all four WebSocket types. External callers cannot mutate the discriminator; serde and crate-internal tests unaffected.
